description: Apply the human-visual-verification principle to our OWN tooling. The 7 viewers in WrkstrmReleaseArtifactUI shipped with 9/9 instantiation tests passing — but instantiation isn't visual confirmation. Before building release-verification-ui-package or release-verification-notifications on top of these viewers, scaffold a small macOS preview app (proposed slug `release-artifact-viewer-preview` under `wrkstrm-components/private/release-artifact-ui/demo-apps/`) that loads hello-world-google v1.0.0-b1's actual receipt JSONs and renders each viewer in all three modes (compact / preview / full). Operator launches it, sees the renders with their own eyes, then signs off via a HumanVisualVerification entry. Refusing to do this would be hypocritical given the vapor-wares.com lesson — automated PASS isn't sufficient for user-visible UI either, and these viewers ARE the user-visible UI for the verification flow. Bead surfaced from the late-evening 2026-05-15 chronicle entry where Claude offered the preview-app loop to the operator at end of session.
title: Scaffold a tiny SwiftUI preview app so the operator SEES every WrkstrmReleaseArtifactUI viewer before the verification flow stacks on top
